title: juncus articulatus subsp. sylvaticus
intro: Juncus articulatus subsp. sylvaticus, also known as the woodland rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Juncaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Juncus articulatus subsp. sylvaticus include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote respiratory health and relieve symptoms associated with colds and flu.
